timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Scottie reveals she left school; Matt arrives, urging Alexandra to see her mom, who she angrily rejects by throwing her golf club; Matt reflects on women in his life self-destructing.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: rebellious daughter confronted by father
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Alexandra curses at the mention of her mother and throws a golf club after her father tells her to come home and see Mom.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Hostile and uncooperative, having rejected the request and continued risky behavior.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Her refusal to see her mother and her engagement in underage drinking and nighttime recklessness.
evidence_quotes: ALEXANDRA Fuck Mom!
Alexandra hears this, takes a few strides, flings her golf club into the night.
GIRLS hitting golf balls -- ALEXANDRA KING and friend EMILY.

relation_updates: strained relationship with mother expressed through anger
status_updates: engaging in underage drinking and risky nighttime behavior
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Teenager expressing anger through profanity and destructive actions.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Matt informs Alexandra that her mother will not wake up and they must let her go as per her will; Alexandra reacts emotionally, upset by the news and how it was delivered.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: daughter receiving news of mother's terminal condition from Matt
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Matt tells Alexandra her mother will not wake up and doctors will stop caring for her; Alexandra reacts by swimming underwater, emerging crying, and rejecting Matt's comfort.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: upset and withdrawn, having left the pool area after rejecting Matt's attempt to console her
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: coping with her mother's impending death
evidence_quotes: They just told me she isn't going to wake up. It's for sure now. The doctors are going to stop caring for her.
Why'd you have to tell me in the goddamned pool?
What do you want?

relation_updates: relationship with Matt becomes strained as she rejects his comfort
status_updates: aware that her mother is going to die
auxiliary.persona_anchor: emotionally vulnerable upon facing loss

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Alexandra saw her mother with a man who had his hand on her ass, confronted her, and cut ties after her mother denied it; Matt suspects Scottie.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: daughter revealing mother's infidelity
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Alexandra tells Matt that her mother was cheating on him, which caused their fight at Christmas and led to her estrangement from her mother.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Matt stands up, walks in a circle, and leaves the room, telling Alexandra to watch Scottie.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how Matt will process the news of his wife's infidelity
evidence_quotes: Dad, she was cheating on you.
He had his hand on her ass. It was gross.
Watch Scottie.

relation_updates: Matt learns about the affair; relationship with mother revealed as broken due to infidelity
status_updates: Matt now knows about the mother's infidelity
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Alexandra as the informant of her mother's affair

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Matt insists Sid shouldn't join the family visit, calling it a private matter, while Alexandra argues Sid's presence helps her behave, and Sid remains indifferent.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: daughter insisting friend Sid accompany her to grandparents' visit
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Alexandra tells Matt that Sid is coming with them to the grandparents' house, arguing she will be more civil with him there, but Matt refuses, calling it a family matter.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to have Sid join her for support during the family visit
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Matt denies Sid's inclusion, and Alexandra continues to advocate for him
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Sid will be allowed to join the family visit
evidence_quotes: ALEXANDRA Sid's coming with us, okay?
ALEXANDRA Dad, I told you he was going to be with me. I'll be a lot more civil with him around.

relation_updates: shows reliance on Sid for support
conflict with Matt over family boundaries
status_updates: Sid introduced as significant friend
auxiliary.persona_anchor: assertive in insisting on peer support

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Alice, confused and believing her deceased daughter is a queen in the hospital, worries about looking presentable; Scott gently indulges her delusion while reminiscing, then violently confronts Sid for laughing at her.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: granddaughter criticized by grandfather
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Scott criticizes Alexandra for causing her mother distress and tells her to be more like Elizabeth; Alexandra shows affection to grandmother Alice by hugging her.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: shamed by her grandfather
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: strained relationship with Scott
evidence_quotes: ALEXANDRA (hugging her) Hi, Tutu.

relation_updates: Scott criticizes Alexandra's past behavior
Alexandra shows affection to Alice
status_updates: shamed by her grandfather
auxiliary.persona_anchor: facing direct criticism from grandfather

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Alexandra identifies Brian Speer from a 'FOR SALE' sign; his polished appearance contrasts Matt's fatigue. Sid jokes about affairs and empty houses, prompting tension. Alexandra takes a photo of Brian's image from the flyer.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: daughter identifying mother's lover
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Alexandra directs Matt to stop the car, points out Brian Speer's photo on a 'FOR SALE' sign, and takes a phone photo of his image.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to obtain a visual identification of Brian Speer
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: has a phone photo of Brian Speer's image from the real estate sign
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: what to do with the photo or information
evidence_quotes: Alexandra suddenly says, 'Dad, stop!'
Alexandra points toward a house with a 'FOR SALE' sign, directing their attention to the photo of the real estate broker displayed on it.
Alexandra notes that they now finally know what Brian looks like.
Alexandra climbs out of the car to grab a flyer and takes a photo of Brian Speer’s image with her phone.

relation_updates: takes investigative lead over Matt
status_updates: acquires photo of Brian Speer
auxiliary.persona_anchor: takes investigative lead over Matt

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Alex takes a call from realtor Bonnie Tanaka, informing Matt that Mr. Speer is on Kaua'i until the 3rd, with an open house option on Sunday; Alex and Matt are pleased.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: daughter confronting parents at deathbed
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Alexandra sarcastically apologizes to her comatose mother, blaming both parents for her upbringing, which leads Matt to spank her.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Alexandra is spanked by Matt and the confrontation ends with Matt asserting that she has no right to speak that way to her dying mother.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Alexandra's anger toward her dying mother and punitive father remains unresolved.
evidence_quotes: Or what? You going to ground me? Or ship me off to another boarding school? You going to give me a \"time out?\"
Matt grabs his older daughter and SPANKS her.

relation_updates: escalates conflict with Matt to physical punishment
expresses deep resentment toward both parents
status_updates: spanked by Matt for insubordination
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Defiant and confrontational in the face of her mother's impending death and her father's authority.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Matt plans to confront Scottie in person to deliver urgent news and make an impact; Alexandra insists on joining, wanting to leave town together.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Alexandra asks to join Matt's trip to confront Brian Speer
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Alexandra shifts from criticizing Matt's confrontation plan to requesting to accompany him and suggesting they all leave town.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to go with Matt to confront Brian Speer and leave town together
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Matt tells her to stay with Scottie, refusing her request
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Alexandra will be allowed to join Matt or find another way to leave
evidence_quotes: Drop it, Dad. You've got way bigger fish to fry than confronting fucking Brian Speer.
Then I want to go too.
Fuck that. Let's all get out of town.

relation_updates: shifts from opposing to supporting Matt's confrontation plan
auxiliary.persona_anchor: moves from opposition to actively seeking involvement in the plan

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: At a cottage built by Alex's relatives, Matt reminisces about childhood visits; Alex suggests Brian show Matt around, prompting a tense, reluctant interaction as they enter.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: daughter confronting her mother's lover alongside her father
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Alexandra accompanies Matt to the Speer cottage and directly questions Brian Speer about the affair, asking if he's sorry her mom is dying and sorry he slept with her and hurt her dad.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to confront Brian Speer with the news of Elizabeth's condition and demand accountability
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Brian Speer apologizes for everything; Alexandra remains seated watching the confrontation unfold
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether the confrontation provides any closure for Alexandra
evidence_quotes: You're sorry my mom's going to die? You're sorry you fucked her? You're sorry you fucked over my dad?
I'm sorry for all of it.
Alex is glad to keep her ringside seat for the moment.
We're not here to hurt you. We just thought she'd want you to know.

relation_updates: establishes hostile relationship with Brian Speer
status_updates: engages in emotional confrontation as key participant
auxiliary.persona_anchor: direct and cutting in confronting the man who hurt her family

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Scottie grapples with her mother Elizabeth's impending death, questioning the process of cremation, while her sister and Sid offer comfort as Dr. Johnston confirms the end is near.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: older sister providing physical and verbal comfort
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Alexandra tells Scottie that removing life support is for the best and physically embraces her after Scottie asks a disturbing question about cremation.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to comfort Scottie during the discussion of their mother's impending death
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Scottie is in Alexandra's arms, having asked about cremation
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Scottie's question about whether eyeballs burn during cremation
evidence_quotes: ALEXANDRA It's for the best, Scottie. Look at her. She's not happy like this.
ALEXANDRA Come here, Scottie.
Scottie goes to her sister, who takes her in her arms.

relation_updates: provides physical comfort to Scottie
offers reassurance about medical decisions
auxiliary.persona_anchor: protective and nurturing towards Scottie

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Matt scatters his wife Elizabeth's ashes into the ocean from a canoe, sharing a solemn moment with his daughters, who join him in releasing leis as a final tribute.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: first to scatter ashes and guide sister in ritual
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Alexandra scatters the first scoop of her mother's ashes and instructs Scottie to say goodbye.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: has completed scattering ashes and passed the urn to Scottie
evidence_quotes: Alex passes the box and scooper to Scottie.
Just say goodbye.

relation_updates: participates in final goodbye with family
status_updates: performs lead role in mourning ritual
auxiliary.persona_anchor: initiates ash-scattering and provides guidance to sister

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Scottie, Matt, and Alexandra share a quiet moment on the sofa, watching TV under a quilt from Scottie's mother's hospital bed, as credits roll.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: joins Matt and Scottie on sofa
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Alexandra enters, sits next to Matt, pulls the quilt over her legs, and asks for ice cream.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Alexandra is seated with Matt and Scottie under the quilt, watching TV as credits roll.
evidence_quotes: Alexandra wanders in, notices what they're watching, sits next to Matt, pulls the quilt over her legs.
ALEXANDRA Can I have some?
Matt hands Alex his ice cream.

relation_updates: reconnects with Matt and Scottie in daily life
status_updates: settles into post-loss family routine
auxiliary.persona_anchor: participates in shared family moment

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Alexandra begins as a rebellious and angry teenager, estranged from her mother. After learning of her mother's terminal illness and infidelity, she becomes actively involved with her father Matt in investigating and confronting the affair. Throughout her mother's decline and death, she takes on protective roles for her sister Scottie and participates in mourning rituals, moving towards acceptance and a new family normal.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Matt King reflects on his wife Elizabeth's severe coma after a powerboat accident, noting her competitive nature and the grim medical prognosis in a Hawaiian hospital.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Husband managing work and hospital care for comatose wife
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Matt is in the hospital room with documents, reflecting on Elizabeth's severe coma from a powerboat accident 23 days ago.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Matt is seated at a makeshift desk in the hospital room, surrounded by work documents, while Elizabeth lies comatose with medical equipment.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Elizabeth's extremely severe coma and Matt's ability to cope with her condition and his responsibilities.
evidence_quotes: Now she's in a coma that scores 5 on the Glasgow scale and 3 on the Rancho Los Amigos scale, scores showing an extremely severe coma.

relation_updates: Elizabeth is comatose and hospitalized
status_updates: Matt is now managing work and hospital care for his wife
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Husband balancing work and vigil at wife's hospital bedside

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: School staff urge Matt to discuss family crisis with Scottie, who acts out by singing profanities at school, showing disengagement and lack of parental intervention.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Father confronted by school staff about daughter's behavior
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Matt is told Scottie has been sharing inappropriate hospital photos and acting out at school; he falsely claims he has been encouraging her to express feelings; he then sees her singing profanities outside the classroom.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Matt is confronted with evidence of Scottie's distress and his lack of intervention, and the school recommends she stay home.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How to handle Scottie's emotional needs and behavior, given Matt's disengagement.
evidence_quotes: MS. HAYASHI We just don't think these photographs are appropriate for Scottie to be sharing with her classmates.
MATT (No) Oh, yeah. Yeah. Absolutely.
They look over to see -- TEN-YEAR-OLD SCOTTIE KING -- EARBUDS in place and in her own world, DANCING just outside the classroom door.

relation_updates: school staff recommend Scottie stay home during crisis
Matt lies about engaging Scottie emotionally
status_updates: confronted with evidence of Scottie's distress and his lack of intervention
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Father struggling to manage parenting during a family crisis.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Dr. Johnston tells Matt to prepare for imminent organ donation and to inform Elizabeth's loved ones, as she will likely pass within days to two weeks after life support withdrawal.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Husband receiving terminal prognosis for comatose wife
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Dr. Johnston informs Matt that Elizabeth's brain damage is irreversible with no brainstem reflexes, and he must prepare for life support withdrawal, organ donation, and notifying her friends.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To arrange organ donation and inform Elizabeth's loved ones before life support removal.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Matt is tasked with starting organ donation procedures and notifying Elizabeth's friends as life support removal is scheduled within days to two weeks.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How to notify Elizabeth's extensive social circle and prepare their daughters for her death.
evidence_quotes: She'll never be like she was, Matt. Never. * We know that now.
DR. JOHNSTON Not if, Matt. When. I have a legal obligation. You know that.
I know I need to get the ball rolling on organ donations right away.

relation_updates: Dr. Johnston delivers definitive terminal prognosis as family friend
status_updates: Elizabeth's condition officially terminal with no recovery possible
Life support removal imminent (days to two weeks)
Organ donation process initiated
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Decision-maker for wife's end-of-life care.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Alexandra saw her mother with a man who had his hand on her ass, confronted her, and cut ties after her mother denied it; Matt suspects Scottie.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Father and husband managing family crisis
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Matt learns from Alexandra that his wife was having an affair, based on Alexandra's account of seeing her mother with another man who had his hand on her ass.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Matt stands up, paces, and leaves the room after telling Alexandra to watch Scottie.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Matt must now deal with his wife's infidelity while managing her terminal condition and family responsibilities.

Matt's relationship with Alexandra becomes strained as he processes her revelation and her earlier disgust at his unawareness.
status_updates: Matt transitions from being an unaware husband to one confronted with evidence of his wife's betrayal.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Matt learns his wife was in love with another man and planned to divorce him; though stunned, he feels relief, chooses not to know the lover's identity, and leaves.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Husband confronting friends about wife's affair
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Matt confirms with Mark and Kai that Elizabeth's affair was ongoing at the time of her accident and that they were aware of it.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To learn the identity of Elizabeth's lover and whether she loved him
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Matt is angry and confrontational, having confirmed the affair's continuity but not the lover's identity.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Who is Elizabeth's lover?
evidence_quotes: Who is he? (off their silence) Does she love him? Who is he?
Was it still going on when she had the accident? Kai looks away. Mark nods.

relation_updates: Matt feels betrayed by Mark and Kai for keeping the affair secret
Matt's friendship with Mark and Kai becomes strained
status_updates: Matt's understanding of his marriage is shattered

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Brian admits to Matt that the affair with Julie was physical, not emotional, and expresses regret; Matt reveals Julie is hospitalized, urging Brian to say goodbye, while showing sorrow.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: interrogator and emotional reactor
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Matt presses Brian for details about the affair and learns it was primarily physical; Brian expresses regret; Matt reveals Julie is hospitalized and his face slackens into overwhelming sadness.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Matt has confronted Brian, received confirmation the affair was physical not emotional, informed Brian that Julie is in Queen's Hospital, and is visibly overcome with grief.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Matt's grief and how to move forward remain unresolved
evidence_quotes: It was an affair, an attraction. Sex.
she's in Queen's Hospital if you want to say goodbye
Matt's face slackens into overwhelming sadness.

relation_updates: Relationship with Brian Speer defined by mutual regret
Matt gains clarity on the affair's nature
status_updates: Matt is emotionally affected and confronts his grief

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 113、EXT. KING FAMILY SHARED PROPERTY HOUSE - LATER

timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Matt has blocked the sale and has seven years to find an alternative solution

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Matt scatters his wife Elizabeth's ashes into the ocean from a canoe, sharing a solemn moment with his daughters, who join him in releasing leis as a final tribute.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Matt King's trajectory begins with his wife Elizabeth in a coma, forcing him into single parenthood and trustee duties for ancestral land. He discovers Elizabeth's affair, confronts her lover Brian Speer, and learns the affair was connected to his family's land sale. Ultimately, he refuses to sell the land, says goodbye to Elizabeth, and scatters her ashes with his daughters, accepting his new role as widower and sole parent.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Reina departs, and Scottie is left without the peer connection she sought.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Scottie's need for peer support during her mother's illness remains unfulfilled.
evidence_quotes: SCOTTIE (looking at her phone) Reina just texted me! She's here visiting her grandma! Can she come visit?
SCOTTIE I need her. Alex needs Sid. I need Reina.
LATER -- Scottie pushes open the door, now accompanied by her friend REINA, 12, who looks around as if the room is dirty.
SCOTTIE Don't you want to hang out? REINA No, I should go. I'll see you at the club. Hope your mom gets better. Exit Reina.

relation_updates: defies Matt's authority by bringing Reina without permission
experiences rejection when Reina leaves uncomforted
status_updates: brings peer into family crisis setting
auxiliary.persona_anchor: child attempting to maintain peer connections in a family crisis setting

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Scottie grapples with her mother Elizabeth's impending death, questioning the process of cremation, while her sister and Sid offer comfort as Dr. Johnston confirms the end is near.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: daughter being directly told by Dr. Herman and her father that her mother will die soon
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Scottie is informed by Dr. Herman and her father that her mother is going to die very soon, and she is given a rubber octopus as a comfort object.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: holding the rubber octopus and confirming with Dr. Johnston that her mother will die
evidence_quotes: DR. HERMAN (CONT'D) And I've heard your mom's not doing too well and that she's going to die very soon.
SCOTTIE Dad, is that true? MATT Yes, Scottie. It's true.
SCOTTIE So Mom's going to die for sure? DR. JOHNSTON Yes.
Dr. Herman hands her a little squeaky RUBBER OCTOPUS she pulls from her pocket.

relation_updates: Dr. Herman establishes a supportive connection with Scottie
status_updates: becomes aware of her mother's terminal condition
auxiliary.persona_anchor: child receiving terminal news while holding a rubber octopus

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Matt scatters his wife Elizabeth's ashes into the ocean from a canoe, sharing a solemn moment with his daughters, who join him in releasing leis as a final tribute.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: daughter scattering her mother's ashes in the ocean
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Scottie scatters a scoop of ashes and says 'Bye, Mom.' to her mother.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: has completed her turn in scattering the ashes and said goodbye to her mother
evidence_quotes: A sad Scottie pours her scoop into the water.

relation_updates: shares solemn ritual with father and sister
status_updates: completes a grief ritual, saying goodbye to her mother
auxiliary.persona_anchor: daughter performing a farewell ritual

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Scottie, Matt, and Alexandra share a quiet moment on the sofa, watching TV under a quilt from Scottie's mother's hospital bed, as credits roll.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: daughter sharing ice cream and a quilt with father and sister on sofa
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Scottie receives ice cream from Matt and shares a quilt with her family as they watch TV together.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: on the sofa with Matt and Alexandra, watching TV as credits roll
evidence_quotes: Scottie lies on a sofa watching television, draped with the QUILT that lay atop her mother's hospital bed.
MATT One scoop strawberry, one scoop mocha chip.
SCOTTIE Great.
the CREDITS roll quietly over them.

relation_updates: family shares intimate moment on sofa, strengthening bonds
status_updates: settles into post-funeral family life with comfort
auxiliary.persona_anchor: child sharing a quiet moment with family under a quilt from her mother's hospital bed

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Scottie, a ten-year-old girl, exhibits behavioral distress and avoidance following her mother's coma, acting out at school and resisting communication with her hospitalized mother. She faces disciplinary issues including cyberbullying, leading to forced apologies and conditional privileges. As her mother's condition becomes terminal, Scottie transitions from denial and anger to participating in farewell rituals and settling into a new family dynamic with her father and sister.
